title: hymenocallis adnata var. littoralis
intro: Hymenocallis adnata var. littoralis, also known as the Coastal Spider Lily, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is native to the coastal regions of the southeastern United States and is known for its large, white flowers that bloom in the summer months. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Hymenocallis adnata var. littoralis can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
